Inflation Signal
← Back to dashboard

Inflation Signal

Broad U.S. inflation signal from price-level dynamics.

As of 2026-02-28

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given an inflation_signal of -0.0029 and an inflation_signal_0_100 score of 20.45, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not Trend continuation.
  • The signal is classified as COOL (disinflation/deflation risk), representing a significant move toward the lower boundary of historical pressure averages (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime Mapping: HOT is defined as z(S) ≥ +0.50, COOL as z(S) ≤ -0.50, and TRANSITION as ±0.25 (1).
  • Component Transformation: Inputs like CPI and M2 are transformed via 120-month rolling robust z-scores to ensure comparability (1).
  • Weighting: Core CPI and 10Y Breakevens are the primary drivers, with auxiliary weights for liquidity and USD (1).
  • Latest Observation: February 28, 2026.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the collapse in the composite z-score to -0.002, moving the signal from NEUTRAL into the COOL regime boundary (1).
  • Stabilisation is not yet evident, as the 0-100 scaled score has dropped from ~26 in early 2026 to 20.45 (1).
  • Internal Conflict: The current z-score (-0.002) technically sits within the TRANSITION band (|z| ≤ 0.25) per rule 6, yet the provided state is labelled COOL (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A reversal in the composite score to z(S) > +0.25.

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Disinflationary drift remains the prevailing macro pressure.
  • Most plausible upside risk: A sharp rebound in 3-month annualised CPI momentum.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Deflationary impulse triggered by a sudden contraction in M2 liquidity or USD strength.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as it captures rolling 120-month standardisations.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, given the internal conflict between the regime label and the transition-zone z-score.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension: Pricing and monetary liquidity impulse (1).
  • Cycle position: Late-cycle disinflation or transition to a cooling regime.
  • Typical interaction: High correlations with Fed policy stance and USD-inverted YoY shifts.

Data & References:

  • US Inflation Signal: Latest observation February 28, 2026 (1).
  • Influential Datapoints: Composite z-score (-0.002) and Regime Label (COOL).
  • Public datasets for depth: BLS Consumer Price Index and FRED M2 Money Stock.
